_metadata.note: Written to repository root because the .geninsights/ directory could not be auto-created by the agent runtime. Intended location is .geninsights/analysis/analysis_results.json.

files.summary: A single-page Streamlit web application that provides a simple four-operation arithmetic calculator (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide) via a clean form-based UI.
files.description: app.py is the sole executable entry point of this project. It renders a Streamlit web application titled 'Simple Calculator' with a centred layout. The user enters two floating-point numbers with six-decimal precision, selects one of four arithmetic operations from a dropdown, and submits the form. The application enforces a hard business rule against division by zero, halting execution with a user-friendly error message in that scenario. On successful computation the result is displayed in a success banner, and an expandable 'Computation details' section exposes the raw operands, chosen operation, and computed result as a structured dictionary for transparency and debugging.
key_responsibilities: Rendering the calculator UI (page config, title, caption, two-column number inputs, operation selector, submit button)
Performing arithmetic calculations for Add, Subtract, Multiply, and Divide operations
Enforcing the division-by-zero guard rule and halting execution cleanly
Displaying the result with a human-readable equation string using Unicode mathematical symbols
Providing an expandable computation details panel for transparency
business_rules: Division by zero is not allowed — if the second operand is 0 and the selected operation is Divide, execution stops and an error message 'Division by zero is not allowed.' is shown to the user
Both number inputs default to 0.0 and accept floating-point values with up to 6 decimal places of precision
The default operation is 'Add' (index 0 in the selectbox)
Results are rendered only after explicit form submission (the Calculate button must be pressed)
The result equation is displayed using Unicode symbols: + for Add, − for Subtract, × for Multiply, ÷ for Divide

methods.name: page_configuration_block
methods.purpose: Configures the Streamlit page with title 'Calculator', a calculator emoji favicon, and centred layout. Also renders the application heading and caption.
methods.significance: Establishes the visual identity and layout of the application before any user interaction.

methods.name: calculator_form_block
methods.return_type: None (renders Streamlit widgets, captures submitted flag)
methods.purpose: Defines the st.form named 'calculator_form' containing two column-aligned number inputs, an operation selectbox, and a submit button. Returns the submitted boolean state.
methods.significance: Central input capture mechanism; the form batches all inputs so the calculation only fires on deliberate submission, preventing partial recalculation on each widget change.

methods.name: calculation_and_output_block
parameters: num1: float — first operand captured from number_input
num2: float — second operand captured from number_input
submitted: bool — True when the form's submit button was clicked
methods.purpose: Executes only when submitted is True. Dispatches to the appropriate arithmetic operation via if/elif/else branching, enforces the division-by-zero rule, sets the result and display symbol, and renders a success message with the equation. Also renders an expander with the structured computation detail dictionary.
methods.significance: Contains all business logic: operation dispatch, error handling, result formatting, and result display. This is the functional core of the application.

method_breakdown.summary: Sets browser tab title to 'Calculator' with a 🧮 icon, uses centred layout, and renders the h1 heading and descriptive caption.
method_breakdown.returns: None — side effect: Streamlit page is configured
method_breakdown.significance: First executed code; determines global page appearance before any widget rendering.

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a Streamlit form with two side-by-side number inputs (First number, Second number) and an Operation dropdown. A 'Calculate' submit button finalises the form. The form prevents Streamlit from re-running the calculation on every individual widget change.
parameters: num1: float — First number input, default 0.0, format '%.6f'
num2: float — Second number input, default 0.0, format '%.6f'
operation: str — Selected from ('Add', 'Subtract', 'Multiply', 'Divide'), default index 0 (Add)
method_breakdown.returns: submitted: bool — True if the Calculate button was clicked in this run
method_breakdown.significance: Ensures atomicity of input collection — all three inputs (num1, num2, operation) are submitted together, preventing race conditions in the reactive Streamlit execution model.

method_breakdown.summary: Guarded by the submitted flag. Branches on the operation string to compute result and assign the display symbol. For 'Divide', checks num2 == 0 and calls st.error() + st.stop() to halt execution with a user-facing error. On success, displays result via st.success() with a formatted equation string, then shows an expandable details dictionary.
parameters: num2: float — second operand (validated against zero for division)
operation: str — determines which arithmetic branch executes
method_breakdown.returns: None — side effects: st.error/st.stop on division-by-zero; st.success + st.expander on success
method_breakdown.significance: This is the primary business logic block. It encapsulates all four arithmetic operations, the sole business validation rule (division-by-zero guard), and the dual output modes (error path vs. success path with detail expansion).

data_model_analysis.entity_definitions: No formal entity or data model classes. Data is handled as bare Python primitives: num1 (float), num2 (float), operation (str), result (float), symbol (str).
data_model_analysis.field_constraints: num1 and num2: floating-point with 6-decimal precision display. num2 must not be 0 when operation is 'Divide'. operation is constrained to one of: 'Add', 'Subtract', 'Multiply', 'Divide'.

validation_rules.input_validation_patterns: Streamlit number_input enforces numeric-only entry. No explicit min/max bounds are set, so any finite float is accepted.
validation_rules.business_validation_logic: Division-by-zero check: `if num2 == 0` when operation is 'Divide' — triggers st.error('Division by zero is not allowed.') and st.stop(), aborting the remainder of the script execution.
validation_rules.constraint_violation_handling: On constraint violation (division by zero), st.error() renders a red error banner and st.stop() immediately halts Streamlit's script execution. No exception is raised; the stop mechanism is Streamlit-native and user-friendly.

external_integrations.third_party_service_calls: None. All computation is local in-process Python arithmetic.
external_integrations.api_consumption_patterns: None. The application is entirely self-contained with no HTTP calls, database queries, or external service dependencies at runtime.

files.summary: Python package dependency manifest specifying a single runtime dependency: streamlit>=1.40.0.
files.description: requirements.txt is the standard pip dependency file for this project. It declares exactly one dependency: streamlit with a minimum version constraint of 1.40.0. This ensures the application runs with a modern Streamlit release that supports the st.form, st.columns, and related APIs used in app.py. No other runtime, test, or development dependencies are listed.
key_responsibilities: Declaring the minimum Streamlit version required to run app.py
Enabling repeatable environment setup via `pip install -r requirements.txt`

external_integrations.api_consumption_patterns: pip install resolves the streamlit package from PyPI using the >=1.40.0 version specifier.

files.summary: Project README providing concise setup instructions (virtual environment creation, dependency installation) and the command to launch the Streamlit calculator application.
files.description: README.md serves as the primary human-facing documentation for this repository. It describes the project as 'a simple calculator web app built with Streamlit' and provides step-by-step setup instructions: creating and activating a Python virtual environment, installing dependencies via pip, and running the application with `streamlit run app.py`. It notes the default local URL (http://localhost:8501). The file contains no architecture decisions, API documentation, or business-rule descriptions.
key_responsibilities: Onboarding developers and users by explaining how to set up and run the application
Documenting the launch command and expected local access URL
